FIELD NOTES · Trail Journal № 01 / 2026
Issue One · Bavaria · 18.04.2026

A spring day at Bad Tölz

An eight-kilometre walk along the Isar, with a brunch in the old town, a picnic by the water, and a baroque chapel filled with uneasy saints.

Abstract

"One of those first real spring days where you almost don't believe it." A short account of a Saturday spent on foot in the Isar valley with family — recorded in six chapters, ten photographs, and one continuous GPS track.

Distance
8.33km
Duration
4h 16m
Ascent
172m
Elevation
636–710m
i.The Route
ii.The Day, in Six Chapters
I
Bad Tölz, Marktstraße
12:25

Arrival

We drove out to Bad Tölz on a Saturday that had decided to be perfect. Sun high, sky clean — one of those first real spring days where you almost don't believe it. We pulled into the parking spot a little after noon, stretched our legs, hoisted little Danny into the carrier, and walked straight into the old town.

II
An old town café
12:40

Brunch

Brunch came first. We found a tiny corner café tucked between the pastel Tölz houses, ordered too much, and let the morning dissolve. Fruit bowls, croissants still warm, a cappuccino with a heart drawn in the foam. Every passerby stopped to say hello to Danny. He took the attention like a pro.

III
Isar riverside path
13:30

Along the Isar

Then the trail. We followed the Isar out of town — that turquoise alpine colour the river gets in spring, almost impossibly green. The path was soft underfoot, pine trees leaning over the water, the occasional family of ducks pretending we weren't there. The further we walked, the quieter the world got.

IV
Picnic, Isarauen
14:30

By the water

We found our picnic spot where the river widens out — mountains on the far shore, just enough breeze to matter. Blanket down, shoes off, feet in the air. Danny had tummy time on the green mat with his little toy chicken. Someone found a bar and did pull-ups. Someone else didn't.

V
The hill chapel
15:30

Kalvarienberg

On the way back we climbed up to the Kalvarienberg. The church is beautiful from the outside — baroque, white, quiet. Inside was a different story. Wax figures of Jesus and the disciples, set into little alcoves, watching you from the shadows as you walked past. Beautiful and a bit unsettling, in equal measure.

VI
By the river
16:30

Dinner

We ended the day the only sensible way: Asian takeaway, eaten by the water, still warm from the walk. Four hours, eight kilometres, one very small boy, and a day that will be easy to remember.

iii.All Plates
